title: "mplayer and dual display"
date: 2008-09-18
forum: New to Ubuntu

### Post by Valk01 on 2008-09-18
so got all up and running and got my second display to more or less do as its supposed to, but when i try to run a mplayer file on the second display, it will fullscreen, but doesnt actully. 
I think since my displays are different res, its trying to fullscreen assuming the second display has the same res?

primary is 1680x1050 and the secondary is 1360x768. is thee a way to make this work?


### Post by yuchai on 2009-03-09
I have the exact same problem with my setup. Primary is a LCD monitor capable of 1920x1200, secondary is an old school CRT TV hooked up through s-video with max capable resolution of 1024x768.

When I run mplayer fullscreen on the TV to play a video, the video is actually expanded to a 1920x1200 scale, so the TV is only able to display the upper left most portion of the full picture.

I've never found a solution for this problem. For now I'm just using 1024x768 on both displays so that the fullscreen video would work on the TV. There has to be a way to fix this but so far I haven't been able to find the solution.

I've tried many things and one of the things I've determined is that this is not an mplayer problem, as any other application that uses fullscreen (such as mythtv) also "thinks" that the resolution is 1920x1200 on the TV. Full screen would work fine, but again only shows the upper left most portion, up to a 1024x768 area.

I see that no one has responded to your inquiry as well, but I was wondering if you found a solution?

---

### Post by ariel on 2009-04-12
Instead of configuring two different screens, you may want to try Twinview.

I've used it for years with a similar setup (LCD panel and TV-out). These days instead of TVout I'm using two DVI outputs, one for my computer monitor and the other for my lcd-tv.

The relevant section in xorg.conf looks like the below. My lcd tv is in dfp-1 and has a resolution of 1920x1080  (in the past I used TV-0 with 1024x768 as resolution).

To turn on or off DFP-1 (or TV-out), you can use xrandr. You will see that with this configuration xrandr will detect two modes; one is DFP-0 only, the other is the twinview mode. 

With mplayer, to go full screen in the second monitor, drag you window to the second monitor and press "f".



Section "Device"
    Identifier    "nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8600 GT]"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Busid        "PCI:1:0:0"

    Option        "AddARGBGLXVisuals"    "True"
    Option         "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
    Option         "RenderAccel"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"

        Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP-0, DFP-1"   

        Option "TwinView" "True"
        Option "TwinViewOrientation" "DFP-0 Above DFP-1"  #LeftOf, Above, etc

    Option "MetaModes" "DFP-0: 1920x1200, DFP-1: NULL; DFP-0: 1920x1200, DFP-1: 1920x1080"

EndSection

---
